parsed_review.summary: CP00-465 is a descriptor-only analytics-core (RP15) closeout pack covering 150 planned units RP15.P05.M06.S06-RP15.P06.M03.S14. Independent read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the pack-level claims are supported. (1) Implementation is real and complete: createAnalyticsCoreCp465P05CloseoutP06FoundationCaseSet/Descriptor (service.js:1598,1631), validateAnalyticsCoreCp465Coverage / validateAnalyticsCoreCp465P05CloseoutP06FoundationDescriptor / Hermes / Claude / Handoff functions (validators.js:2233-2378); all 7 required_public_exports exist and are re-exported via `export *` (index.js). (2) Counts are internally consistent at 150 across the prompt baseline, requirements descriptor (registry.js:1271-1345), manifest (delivery_counts/micro_phase_row_counts), and the validator; deliverable distribution {fixture:29,claude_review:8,implementation:59,security_audit:22,test:16,hermes_evidence:4,ui:12} and micro-title distribution match exactly. (3) No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code via freezeCp465Result (service.js:1550 sets no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, dispatches_*:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true) and attested in the manifest implementation_summary block (manifest.json:5915-5964) and the no_write_attestation chain, with no unsafe `true` overrides found. (4) Authority boundary is correct: descriptor authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true (runtime opening deferred to CP00-466); manifest claude_not_final_approver:true, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation:true. (5) Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic control-plane evidence packet/receipt (no_hermes_runtime_receipt:true; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false), and the Claude review packet is read_only:true with allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob and invalid_review_blockers. (6) The pack does not over-claim: production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, status implemented_pending_review; no `production_ready:true`/`writes_product_state:true`/`no_real_data:false` anywhere in the manifest. (7) Tests/validation: 3 dedicated CP465 tests (model.test.js:850-906) plus ~35 CP465 assertions in scripts/validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs, and contract projections (cp465_requirements, cp465_no_write_attestation, p05_closeout_p06_foundation_descriptor, current_pack, historical_pack_bindings) are present and asserted; command-evidence records all pre-review commands at exit 0, with closeout-pack:validate correctly deferred as post-review gated. (8) Review-hardening pipeline is healthy: auth preflight passed with non-zero, well-formed JSON stdout (readiness-report.json), no invalid attempts recorded for this pack, and validation-report confirms no forbidden tools and no omitted active untracked implementation files. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer, not the final approver; the enterprise trust claim and final approval are closed by a human outside this receipt. No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One trivial non-blocking P3 observation on the changed-file-scope labeling. Pack closeout may proceed.

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists the pack's own manifest dir and handoff doc in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-465/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145). Semantically a file should not be both an active implementation/untracked-deliverable file and 'unrelated'. This is benign: the authoritative review scope is active_pack_implementation_refs (the 8 tracked source files: contracts/analytics-core-contract.json, packages/analytics/README.md, src/index.js, src/registry.js, src/service.js, src/validators.js, test/model.test.js, scripts/validate-rp15-analytics-core-contract.mjs), and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (confirmed independently in validation-report.json:24), so no active implementation file was hidden from review. The unrelated_dirty_files_preserved bucket is a conservative catch-all of every dirty path not in active_pack_implementation_refs. The genuinely unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 .docx, docs/ldip-integration/.../c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json from a different review track, and the workbook/* matter docs) were correctly not inferred to be part of CP00-465.
findings.recommendation: Optional clarity improvement: exclude active untracked implementation files (the pack manifest dir and handoff doc) from the unrelated_dirty_files_preserved list so the two buckets are mutually exclusive. No functional change required; does not affect scope integrity or the production_ready determination.
findings.post_review_disposition: accepted_non_blocking — cosmetic scope-descriptor labeling only; gating field active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so review coverage is complete and pack closeout may proceed.
